transcript: Did you know that there are about one and a quarter million children like you and me in Ireland? That's a lot of children and all of us have rights. Rights are things that every child needs to live a full and happy life no matter who they are or where they live. That means that children like you and me should have food, clean water, a safe home, go to school and be able to play with their friends as well as lots of other rights. These rights are the same for all children, whether they are a girl or a boy, no matter what colour their skin is, where they come from, what religion they are or if they have a disability. In Ireland and around the world, children have their rights protected by the government. The UNCRC says that children everywhere should have special rights and promises to protect them and keep them safe. My teacher told me that it's everyone's responsibility to make sure that our rights are protected. But there is one person in particular who makes this happen in Ireland. That is Ombudsman for Children Dr Niall Muldoon. The Ombudsman's job is to make sure that people who make laws and decisions think about children's rights. Children can also contact his office if they need service and things aren't working out the way they should. You can call the office for free on 1-800-202-040.
